summary: Iteration 3 backend testing of SkiFi Designs Resources/Templates + Emergent Google Auth integration. 36/37 pytest cases pass. The single failure is a real bug in /api/payments/status/{session_id}: when the session is in 'pending' state and the backend polls Stripe via emergentintegrations.StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status(), a Pydantic validation error is raised because Stripe returns 'metadata' as a StripeObject, but CheckoutStatusResponse model expects dict. This affects ALL one-time payment polling (both template purchases AND per-slide project payments), but only when the tx is still in 'pending' state (already-paid txs short-circuit and never hit the Stripe poll). Verified: auth/me 401 without token, auth/session 400 missing header, auth/session 401 on invalid emergent session, full admin templates CRUD (free + paid create with file_url, reject paid w/price=0 with 400, PATCH updates fields, PATCH unknown→404, PATCH no valid fields→400, DELETE works + verifies removal, DELETE unknown→404, admin list shows file_url), public templates list/get hides file_url, filters by category/type/search all work, /templates/{id}/access correctly 401 without auth, 404 for unknown id even when authenticated (because Path is checked first - which is fine), free template access returns download_url, paid template access creates Stripe checkout successfully (real live stripe key), /me/library 401 without auth, returns empty {items:[]} for new authed user. Regression on /, /admin/login wrong pwd, /admin/submissions, /payments/status unknown 404, /onboarding/create-checkout invalid pkg 400, /webhook/stripe accepts invalid sig with 200 (intentional), /auth/logout — all green.

critical.issue: Returns HTTP 500 'Unexpected error retrieving session status: 1 validation error for CheckoutStatusResponse, metadata Input should be a valid dictionary [type=dict_type, input_value=<StripeObject...>, input_type=StripeObject]' whenever the underlying tx (template OR project) is in 'pending' state and needs to poll Stripe. Root cause: emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutStatusResponse Pydantic model declares metadata: Dict[str,str], but Stripe SDK returns a StripeObject (dict-like but not a dict). This breaks the payment-success page polling flow for users returning from Stripe Checkout. Reproduces 100% in test_payments_status_for_template_session.

minor.issue: request.headers.get('content-length') comparison uses tuple ('0', None) but http clients send content-length as a string. The `await request.json() if request.headers.get('content-length') not in ('0', None) else {}` will crash if the client sends a non-JSON body with content-length>0 (e.g. plain text). Wrap in try/except. Already works in tests because we always send JSON.

On Emergent Auth network failure returns 502 not 401, which is correct, but test had to accept both. Consider distinguishing 'invalid session id' (401) from 'auth provider unreachable' (502) — currently they're conflated when r.status_code != 200.

Does not exclude _id projection on the returned doc — uses find_one({'id': template_id}, {'_id': 0}) which IS correct, but the doc isn't wrapped in _public_template OR explicit type-coercion, so created_at remains a string in PATCH response but a datetime ISO in CREATE response. Mildly inconsistent.
Response merges _public_template(doc) | {'file_url': doc.get('file_url')} but does NOT include is_published. Admin probably wants to see that field on create echo too. Minor.

action_items: HIGH: Fix /api/payments/status/{session_id} 500 error caused by emergentintegrations CheckoutStatusResponse metadata=StripeObject vs dict mismatch. Options: (a) catch the ValidationError and fall back to stripe_sdk.checkout.Session.retrieve() directly (already used for subscription mode); (b) coerce metadata to dict() before passing to the response model; (c) pin/upgrade emergentintegrations to a version that handles StripeObject; (d) wrap CheckoutStatusResponse in a try/except and manually build a response dict from the raw Stripe object. Quickest workaround: replace the get_checkout_status call in /payments/status with a direct stripe_sdk.checkout.Session.retrieve(session_id) and read payment_status/status/metadata from that object. NOTE: This bug is currently invisible because no real user has polled a pending tx yet — but the next time a customer returns from Stripe Checkout, the payment-success page will get 500.
MINOR: Wrap `await request.json()` in /templates/{id}/access in try/except to avoid crashes if body is missing or non-JSON.
MINOR: PATCH /admin/templates return should be consistent with POST response (include file_url, normalize created_at).
critical_code_review_comments: _public_template() correctly strips file_url and _id from public responses — verified.
require_user() supports both httpOnly cookie AND Authorization: Bearer header — good for SPA + API access.
session_token expiry check correctly handles tz-naive datetimes by upgrading to UTC.
/templates/{id}/access correctly does require_user BEFORE template lookup (verified: 401 even for unknown template id when no auth — confirmed by review_request note).
Paid template check: existing 'paid' purchase short-circuits to download_url — but does NOT log a re-download event in template_downloads. Consider logging.

Stripe checkout for template purchase: metadata.kind='template_purchase' is set, but server.py /webhook/stripe and /payments/status both detect 'template' kind by checking which collection has the session_id — works, but relying on metadata.kind would be more robust.
TemplateModel.created_at is datetime, but inserted into Mongo as ISO string via doc['created_at']=doc['created_at'].isoformat() — consistent with rest of codebase.
Admin auth uses Bearer Authorization; user auth uses both Cookie and Bearer — clean separation.
secrets.token_urlsafe(48) for session tokens (384 bits) — strong.
MongoDB _id properly excluded with {'_id': 0} projection in EVERY find/find_one in templates code — verified.
POTENTIAL BUG: emergentintegrations CheckoutStatusResponse metadata=Dict[str,str] vs Stripe SDK returning StripeObject — breaks /payments/status polling. See action items.

Race condition: when user creates a paid template purchase and then refreshes, /access creates a NEW pending purchase row each time (no idempotency check on pending). After 10 refreshes, 10 pending Stripe sessions + 10 db rows. Consider checking for an existing pending purchase < 1 hour old before creating a new one.

/me/library only returns 'paid' purchases — free template downloads are logged to template_downloads but NEVER returned from /me/library. The endpoint description says 'purchased + downloaded' but only purchased is implemented. Minor scope gap.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_templates_and_auth.py (NEW - 37 test cases covering templates + auth + regression)

success_rate.backend: 97.3% (36/37 pytest cases passed; 1 real bug exposed in /api/payments/status)

test_credentials: ADMIN_PASSWORD=skifi-admin-2026 (from /app/backend/.env). User auth uses manually-inserted db.users + db.user_sessions row (see /app/auth_testing.md). Fixture creates TEST_user_xxx + TEST_sess_xxx pair, cleans up on teardown.
seed_data_creation: Created (and cleaned up) per test run: 2 TEST_ templates (free Pitch Deck + paid Sales Deck $49), 1 TEST_ user + user_session, 1 template_download row (free access), 1 template_purchases row (pending, paid template). All cleaned up: free + paid templates deleted via DELETE /admin/templates, TEST_ user/session deleted via mongo fixture teardown. The pending template_purchases row from the paid-access test is NOT cleaned up (no admin endpoint for it) — left in db.template_purchases as TEST_ user_id which the fixture removes via template_purchases.delete_many({user_id: uid}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the /api/payments/status 500 bug, re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_templates_and_auth.py::TestPaidTemplatePurchase -v. Also worth: manually create a paid template_purchase row in mongo with payment_status='pending', then hit /api/payments/status/{that_session_id} and verify it does NOT 500. The bug is in the emergentintegrations library's CheckoutStatusResponse Pydantic model — easiest fix is to replace stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status(session_id) with direct stripe_sdk.checkout.Session.retrieve(session_id) in server.py around line 364. Also consider: emergentintegrations may have a newer version that fixes this — pip show emergentintegrations to check version.
rca of the issue:
BUG: /api/payments/status returns 500 for pending sessions. REPRO: (1) POST /admin/templates create paid template, (2) POST /templates/{id}/access with auth → returns session_id, (3) GET /payments/status/{session_id} immediately (before payment) → HTTP 500 with Pydantic ValidationError. ROOT CAUSE: emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutStatusResponse defines metadata: Dict[str,str], but Stripe Python SDK returns Session.metadata as a stripe.api_resources.abstract.api_resource.StripeObject (which is dict-like but fails isinstance(x,dict) check that Pydantic v2 uses). MITIGATION (server.py line ~363): replace the StripeCheckout polling with direct stripe_sdk usage, e.g.:

    session = stripe_sdk.checkout.Session.retrieve(session_id)
    new_payment_status = session.payment_status or 'pending'
    new_status = session.status or 'open'

This is exactly what the subscription branch already does at line 359, so the fix is to unify both branches to use stripe_sdk directly and drop the StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status call. Alternatively, coerce: `result_data = dict(stripe_session); result_data['metadata'] = dict(result_data.get('metadata') or {}); CheckoutStatusResponse(**result_data)`.
